title: Materials and methods for treatment of hemoglobinopathies
abstract: Materials and methods for treating a patient with hemoglobinopathy, both ex vivo and in vivo and materials and methods for deleting and/or mutating at least a portion of a human beta globin locus on chromosome 11 in a human cell by genome editing and thereby increasing the production of fetal hemoglobin (HbF).
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A method for editing a human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 in a human cell by genome editing, the method comprising the step of: introducing into the human cell one or more deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) endonucleases to effect one or more single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double-strand breaks (DSBs) within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in at least one permanent deletion, at least one mutation, or at least one permanent deletion and at least one mutation within or near the human beta globin locus and results in an increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the human cell. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the at least one permanent deletion within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 is in a region of the human beta globin locus comprising a HBG1 gene and a HBG2 gene. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the at least one permanent deletion within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 is a 13-base pair deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the at least one permanent deletion within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 is a 4.9 Kb deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein the at least one permanent deletion within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 is a 13-base pair deletion located upstream of the HGB1 gene and a 4.9 Kb deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 6. The method of any of claims 4 or 5, wherein the 4.9 Kb deletion includes the HBG2 gene. 7. An ex vivo method for treating a patient with a hemoglobinopathy, the method comprising the steps of: creating a patient specific induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC); editing within or near a beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 of the iPSC; differentiating the genome-edited iPSC into a hematopoietic progenitor cell; and implanting the hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the creating step comprises: isolating a somatic cell from the patient; and introducing a set of pluripotency-associated genes into the somatic cell to induce the somatic cell to become a pluripotent stem cell. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the somatic cell is a fibroblast. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the set of pluripotency-associated genes is one or more of the genes selected from the group consisting of OCT4, SOX2, KLF4, Lin28, NANOG and cMYC. 1 1 . The method of any one of claims 7-10, wherein the editing step comprises introducing into the iPSC one or more deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) endonucleases to effect one or more single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double-strand breaks (DSBs) within or near the beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in at least one permanent deletion, at least one mutation, or at least one permanent deletion and at least one mutation within or near the beta globin locus and results in an increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the iPSC. 12. The method of any one of claims 7-1 1 , wherein the differentiating step comprises one or more of the following to differentiate the genome-edited iPSC into a hematopoietic progenitor cell: treatment with a combination of small molecules, delivery of master transcription factors, delivery of mRNA encoding master transcription factors, or delivery of mRNA encoding transcription factors. 13. The method of any one of claims 7-12, wherein the implanting step comprises implanting the hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient by transplantation, local injection, systemic infusion, or combinations thereof. 14. An ex vivo method for treating a patient with a hemoglobinopathy, the method comprising the steps of: isolating a mesenchymal stem cell from the patient; editing within or near a beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 of the mesenchymal stem cell; differentiating the genome-edited mesenchymal stem cell into a hematopoietic progenitor cell; and implanting the hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the mesenchymal stem cell is isolated from the patient's bone marrow or peripheral blood. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the isolating step comprises: aspiration of bone marrow and isolation of mesenchymal cells using density gradient centrifugation media. 17. The method of any one of claims 14-16, wherein the editing step comprises introducing into the mesenchymal stem cell one or more deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) endonucleases to effect one or more single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double-strand breaks (DSBs) within or near the beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in at least one permanent deletion, at least one mutation, or at least one permanent deletion and at least one mutation within or near the beta globin locus and results in an increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the mesenchymal stem cell. 18. The method of any one of claims 14-17, wherein the differentiating step comprises one or more of the following to differentiate the genome-edited mesenchymal stem cell into a hematopoietic progenitor cell: treatment with a combination of small molecules, delivery of master transcription factors, delivery of mRNA encoding master transcription factors, or delivery of mRNA encoding transcription factors. 19. The method of any one of claims 14-18, wherein the implanting step comprises implanting the hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient by transplantation, local injection, systemic infusion, or combinations thereof. 20. An ex vivo method for treating a patient with a hemoglobinopathy, the method comprising the steps of: isolating a hematopoietic progenitor cell from the patient; editing within or near a beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 of the hematopoietic progenitor cell; and implanting the genome-edited hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient. 21 . The method of claim 20, wherein the method further comprises treating the patient with granulocyte colony stimulating factor (GCSF) prior to the isolating step. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the treating step is performed in combination with Plerixaflor. 23. The method of any one of claims 20-22, wherein the isolating step comprises isolating CD34+ cells. 24. The method of any one of claims 20-23, wherein the editing step comprises introducing into the hematopoietic progenitor cell one or more deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) endonucleases to effect one or more single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double- strand breaks (DSBs) within or near the beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in at least one permanent deletion, at least one mutation, or at least one permanent deletion and at least one mutation within or near the beta globin locus and results in an increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the hematopoietic progenitor cell. 25. The method of any one of claims 20-24, wherein the implanting step comprises implanting the genome-edited hematopoietic progenitor cell into the patient by transplantation, local injection, systemic infusion, or combinations thereof. 26. The method of any one of claims 1 -25, wherein the increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the genome-edited human cells is compared to HbF levels in wild-type human cells. 27. The method of any one of claims 1 -26, wherein the increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) results in the genome-edited human cells having at least 30% HbF. 28. The method of any one of claims 7-27, wherein the hemoglobinopathy is selected from a group consisting of sickle cell anemia and thalassemia (Î±, Î², Î´, Î³, and combinations thereof). 29. An in vivo method for treating a patient with a hemoglobinopathy, the method comprising the step of editing a human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 in a cell of the patient. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the editing step comprises introducing into the cell one or more deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) endonucleases to effect one or more single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double-strand breaks (DSBs) within or near the human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in at least one permanent deletion, at least one mutation, or at least one permanent deletion and at least one mutation within or near the beta globin locus and results in an increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the cell. 31 . The method of any one of claims 29-30, wherein the cell is a bone marrow cell, a hematopoietic progenitor cell, or a CD34+ cell. 32. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24 or 30, wherein the one or more DNA endonucleases is a Cas1 , CasI B, Cas2, Cas3, Cas4, Cas5, Cas6, Cas7, Cas8, Cas9 (also known as Csn1 and Csx12), Casl OO, Csy1 , Csy2, Csy3, Cse1 , Cse2, Csc1 , Csc2, Csa5, Csn2, Csm2, Csm3, Csm4, Csm5, Csm6, Cmr1 , Cmr3, Cmr4, Cmr5, Cmr6, Csb1 , Csb2, Csb3, Csx17, Csx14, Csx10, Csx16, CsaX, Csx3, Csx1 , Csx15, Csf1 , Csf2, Csf3, Csf4, or Cpf1 endonuclease; a homolog thereof, a recombination of 

 the naturally occurring molecule thereof, codon-optimized thereof, or modified versions thereof, and combinations thereof. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the method comprises introducing into the cell one or more polynucleotides encoding the one or more DNA endonucleases. 34. The method of claim 32, wherein the method comprises introducing into the cell one or more ribonucleic acids (RNAs) encoding the one or more DNA endonucleases. 35. The method of any one of claims 33 or 34, wherein the one or more polynucleotides or one or more RNAs is one or more modified polynucleotides or one or more modified RNAs. 36. The method of claim 32, wherein the one or more DNA endonuclease is one or more proteins or polypeptides. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the one or more proteins or polypeptides is flanked at the N-terminus, the C-terminus, or both the N-terminus and C-terminus by one or more nuclear localization signals (NLSs). 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the one or more proteins or polypeptides is flanked by two NLSs, one NLS located at the N-terminus and the second NLS located at the C-terminus. 39. The method of any one of claims 37-38, wherein the one or more NLSs is a SV40 NLS. 40. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises introducing into the cell one or more guide ribonucleic acids (gRNAs). 41 . The method of claim 40, wherein the one or more gRNAs are single-molecule guide RNA (sgRNAs). 42. The method of any one of claims 40-41 , wherein the one or more gRNAs or one or more sgRNAs is one or more modified gRNAs or one or more modified sgRNAs. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein the one or more modified sgRNAs comprises three 2'-0-methyl-phosphorothioate residues at or near each of its 5' and 3' ends. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the modified sgRNA is the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 . 45. The method of any one of claims 40-44, wherein the one or more DNA endonucleases is pre-complexed with one or more gRNAs or one or more sgRNAs to form one or more ribonucleoproteins (RNPs). 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the weight ratio of sgRNA to DNA endonuclease in the RNP is 1 : 1 . 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the sgRNA comprises the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 , the DNA endonuclease is a S. pyogenes Cas9 comprising a N-terminus SV40 NLS and a C-terminus SV40 NLS, wherein the weight ratio of sgRNA to DNA endonuclease is 1 : 1 . 48. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24, or 30, wherein the method further comprises introducing into the cell one or more guide ribonucleic acid (gRNAs), and wherein the one or more DNA endonucleases is one or more Cas9 endonucleases that effect or create a pair of single-strand breaks (SSBs) or double-strand breaks (DSBs), a first SSB or DSB at a 5' locus and a second SSB or DSB at a 3' locus, within or near the beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 that results in a permanent deletion of the chromosomal DNA between the 5' locus and the 3' locus. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the permanent deletion of the chromosomal DNA between the 5' locus and the 3' locus is in a region of the human beta globin locus comprising a HBG1 gene and a HBG2 gene. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein the permanent deletion of the chromosomal DNA between the 5' locus and the 3' locus is a 13-base pair deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 51 . The method of claim 49, wherein the permanent deletion of the chromosomal DNA between the 5' locus and the 3' locus is a 4.9 Kb deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 52. The method of claim 49, wherein the permanent deletion of the chromosomal DNA between the 5' locus and the 3' locus is a 13-base pair deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene and a 4.9 Kb deletion located upstream of the HBG1 gene. 53. The method of any of claims 48-52, wherein one gRNA creates a pair of SSBs or DSBs. 54. The method of any of claims 48-53, wherein one gRNA comprises a spacer sequence that is complementary to either the 5' locus or the 3' locus. 55. The method of any of claims 48-54, wherein the one gRNA is one single- molecule guide RNA (sgRNA). 56. The method of any one of claims 48-55, wherein the one gRNA or one sgRNA is one modified gRNA or one modified sgRNA. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the one modified sgRNA comprises three 2' methyl-phosphorothioate residues at or near each of its 5' and 3' ends. 58. The method of claim 57, wherein the one modified sgRNA is the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 . 59. The method of any one of claims 48-58, wherein the one or more Cas9 endonucleases is pre-complexed with one gRNA or one sgRNA to form one or more ribonucleoproteins (RNPs). 60. The method of claim 59, wherein the one or more Cas9 endonuclease is flanked at the N-terminus, the C-terminus, or both the N-terminus and C-terminus by one or more nuclear localization signals (NLSs). 61 . The method of claim 60, wherein the one or more Cas9 endonucleases is flanked by two NLSs, one NLS located at the N-terminus and the second NLS located at the C- terminus. 62. The method of any one of claims 60-61 , wherein the one or more NLSs is a SV40 NLS. 63. The method of claim 59, wherein the weight ratio of sgRNA to Cas9 endonuclease in the RNP is 1 : 1 . 64. The method of claim 59, wherein the one sgRNA comprises the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 , the Cas9 endonuclease is a S. pyogenes Cas9 comprising a N-terminus SV40 NLS and a C-terminus SV40 NLS, wherein the weight ratio of sgRNA to Cas9 endonuclease is 1 : 1 . 65. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24 or 30-64 wherein the Cas9 mRNA, and gRNA, are either each formulated into separate lipid nanoparticles or all co- formulated into a lipid nanoparticle. 66. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24 or 30-64, wherein the Cas9 mRNA is formulated into a lipid nanoparticle, and the gRNA are delivered to the cell by an adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector. 67. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24 or 30-64, wherein the Cas9 mRNA is formulated into a lipid nanoparticle and the gRNA is delivered to the cell by electroporation. 68. The method of any one of claims 1 , 1 1 , 17, 24, or 30-64, wherein the one or more RNP is delivered to the cell by electroporation. 69. The method of any one of claims 30-68, wherein the increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the genome-edited human cells is compared to HbF levels in wild-type human cells. 70. The method of any one of claims 30-69, wherein the increase of fetal hemoglobin (HbF) in the genome-edited human cell results in the genome-edited human cells having at least 30% HbF. 71 . The method of any one of claims 29-70, wherein the hemoglobinopathy is selected from a group consisting of sickle cell anemia and thalassemia (Î±, Î², Î´, Î³, and combinations thereof). 72. The method of any one of claims 1 -71 , wherein the gRNA or sgRNA comprises a spacer sequence of SEQ ID NO: 139. 73. One or more guide ribonucleic acids (gRNAs) for editing a human beta globin locus on chromosome 1 1 in a cell from a patient with a hemoglobinopathy, the one or more gRNAs comprising a spacer sequence of SEQ ID NO: 139. 74. The one or more gRNAs of claim 73, wherein the one or more gRNAs are one or more single-molecule guide RNAs (sgRNAs). 75. The one or more gRNAs or sgRNAs of claims 73 or 74, wherein the one or more gRNAs or one or more sgRNAs is one or more modified gRNAs or one or more modified sgRNAs. 76. The one or more modified sgRNAs of claim 75, wherein the one or more modified sgRNAs comprises three 2'-O-methyl-phosphorothioate residues at or near each of its 5' and 3' ends. 77. The one or more modified sgRNAs of claim 75, wherein the one or more modified sgRNAs comprises the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 . 78. A single-molecule guide RNA (sgRNA) comprising the nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 201 . 79. The gRNA or sgRNA of any one of claims 73-78, wherein the hemoglobinopathy is selected from a group consisting of sickle cell anemia and thalassemia (Î±, Î², Î´, Î³, and combinations thereof).
